title: Corallodendron caffrum
taxonomy: Corallodendron caffrum is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is native to South Africa, where it is found in rocky areas and scrublands. The genus Corallodendron is part of the larger family Apocynaceae, which includes around 5,000 species of plants. Corallodendron caffrum is further classified as a member of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, which contains plants with showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. The specific classification of Corallodendron caffrum is Corallodendron caffrum (Schltr.) Markgr., indicating its unique place in the botanical hierarchy.

intro: Corallodendron caffrum, also known as the coral tree, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae that is native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Corallodendron caffrum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Corallodendron caffrum is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction to treat various health conditions, and is also used in the form of ointments and creams for topical application.
